title: Classify historical convergence states with bound evidence
goal: Build a bounded, reproducible backfill over historical Grabowski and Bureau attention records using the deployed convergence-state classifier, without rewriting source history, inferring completion or flattening contradictory evidence.

approval.note: Read and classify existing records only. Any later source-state mutation requires a separate reviewed plan and exact task binding.

acceptance.assertion: The backfill selects an explicit bounded record set with source identifiers, observation timestamps and stable content hashes; omitted records and truncation are reported.
Every selected record is evaluated with the deployed convergence-state-classify contract and the exact runtime release, request hash and receipt hash are retained.
Resolved, superseded, blocked, expected and defect signals follow the documented decision table; incompatible terminal signals remain conflicted rather than being silently prioritized.
The backfill produces a separate projection and never edits Bureau task files, queues, operator-obligation history, GitHub state or Chronik receipts.
A classification never establishes task completion, safe retry, root cause, merge readiness or authority to close an operator obligation.
The final summary reports counts by classification, integrity errors, truncation, conflicted records and per-source evidence references in a deterministic machine-readable artifact.
Fixtures cover resolved plus blocked, superseded plus blocked, conflicting terminal signals, missing evidence, invalid records and deterministic reruns.

metadata.source: Post-merge review and closeout of Grabowski PRs #255-#258 and follow-up PR #260.
nonclaims: all attention records are safe to retry
